prose:
The fukui-reactivity calculation for benzaldehyde was performed using Density Functional Theory (DFT) at the tier `standard` level, which corresponds to the **B3LYP functional with the def2-TZVP basis set**, in PySCF. The calculation was performed in the **gas phase** with a **charge of 0** and a **multiplicity of 1**. The geometry was taken from the provided `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/benzaldehyde.xyz` file and was not optimized as part of this calculation. The SCF converged, and the engine's integrity check reported `ok`.

The most electrophilic site, identified by the highest f+ (nucleophilic attack) Fukui index, is **Carbon (C)** at atom index 2 (1-based label 3), with an f+ value of **0.39939**.
The most nucleophilic site, identified by the highest f- (electrophilic attack) Fukui index, is **Oxygen (O)** at atom index 0 (1-based label 1), with an f- value of **0.23840**.

All Fukui indices (f+, f-, and dual descriptor) were computed per atom and are available in the output JSON.
